name: inv_allows_empty_arch
reason: An empty architecture with zero layers should not satisfy the invariant, as a valid MMU arch needs at least one layer

name: inv_allows_unit_num_entries
reason: A multi-layer arch where every layer has num_entries==1 is degenerate — page tables provide no fan-out and the hierarchy is pointless

name: inv_allows_equal_consecutive_entry_sizes
reason: Consecutive layers with identical entry_size means the higher layer doesn't actually subdivide address space, violating the purpose of hierarchical paging

name: entry_size_is_next_layer_size_vacuous_beyond_bounds
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_size_is_next_layer_size_vacuous_beyond_bounds(a: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        a.inv(),
        a.layers.len() > 0,
        i >= a.layers.len(),
    ensures
        a.entry_size_is_next_layer_size(i),
{
}
reason: entry_size_is_next_layer_size is vacuously true for out-of-range indices, which could mask specification errors if callers forget bounds checks

name: inv_allows_top_entry_size_one
reason: A full 4-layer arch whose top-level entry_size is 1 byte maps only 1 byte total — far too small for any real x86 page table hierarchy
